pages.raw_text:
LOOKS GOOD - PAGE ONE 49,.


II the vote in Spokane today Is
any indication of the sentiment of
the state, it Is fairly certain that
woman suffrage will carry.


From all parts of the city comes
the report that the amendment
which will give the ballot is pull
ing a strong vote, and in nearly
every precinct which could be
reached the indications are that
the suffrage proposition will carry.


Good work has been done today
by the earnest women who went
to the polling places to urge the
voters to give them equal rights.
and although none of the workers
have resorted to stump speaking,
or soap boz methods, very few Of
the voters have been allowed to
reach the polling booths before at
least word has been said in
favor of giving women right to
vote.


That this quiet but earnest work
is bearing fruit is evidenced by the
number of voters who are express
sing themselves as in favor of the
movement, either prior to or sub
sequent to casting their ballots.
